title: Mastery Exam: Readable and Responsive Typography
summary: Mastery Exam: Readable and Responsive Typography retrieves prior skills in an original independent typography release decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css font families fallbacks, css web font loading, css type scale, css line measure spacing, css text wrapping overflow, css responsive type in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent typography release decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Volunteer Shift Deck typography work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the resilient typography system here. */

steps.title: 01 · Sequence 1: shift deck preferred-font failure and layout shift audit · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Reconstruct the verification workflow. Scenario: independent typography release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Readable and Responsive Typography makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent typography release decision.

content.text: Order the independent typography release decision work by dependency and verify that each phase creates evidence needed by the next.

hints: Mastery Exam: Readable and Responsive Typography checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Readable and Responsive Typography checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Readable and Responsive Typography checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: independent typography release decision phase 1.1: identify reading roles languages and supported platforms
independent typography release decision phase 1.2: choose preferred faces metric-compatible fallbacks and generic family
independent typography release decision phase 1.3: compare line breaks x-height and control dimensions
independent typography release decision phase 1.4: test blocked slow failed and user-overridden font loading

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 2: one regular font file can synthesize every weight and style with equal quality.
Independent typography release decision case 2: declare resources for actual requested styles and ranges, choose display behavior deliberately, and verify loading plus fallback transitions.
Independent typography release decision case 2: use font-display block for all body text because invisible text prevents layout shift.

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 3: fixed pixel font sizes provide the most consistent accessible typography.
Independent typography release decision case 3: start from user-scalable body text, define a small bounded scale by content role, and test zoom, narrow width, and long headings.
Independent typography release decision case 3: set every heading in pixels so browser text preferences cannot distort the visual brand.

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 4: tightly packed long lines make reading faster because more text fits on screen.
Independent typography release decision case 4: set a readable unitless line height and bounded measure, create paragraph rhythm, and test user text-spacing overrides plus zoom.
Independent typography release decision case 4: increase letter spacing on all paragraphs until every line reaches the same width.

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 5: ellipsis is safe for any content because users understand hidden text exists.

Independent typography release decision case 5: allow natural wrapping, add language-aware hyphenation or emergency token breaks, and keep essential text fully available without fixed-height clipping.
Independent typography release decision case 5: set white-space nowrap and text-overflow ellipsis on every heading to preserve card alignment.

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 7: a downloaded web font displays before it loads with no layout consequences.
Independent typography release decision case 7: choose metric-compatible fallbacks, end with a generic family, and test initial, late, failed, and user-overridden font states.
Independent typography release decision case 7: list only one brand font because browsers synthesize a similar face when it fails.

independent typography release decision phase 26.2: map each request to a real WOFF2 or variable resource
independent typography release decision phase 26.3: choose display behavior and optional preload from measured priority
independent typography release decision phase 26.4: test cache slow blocked failed and late-swap states

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 27: fixed pixel font sizes provide the most consistent accessible typography.
Independent typography release decision case 27: start from user-scalable body text, define a small bounded scale by content role, and test zoom, narrow width, and long headings.
Independent typography release decision case 27: set every heading in pixels so browser text preferences cannot distort the visual brand.

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 28: tightly packed long lines make reading faster because more text fits on screen.
Independent typography release decision case 28: set a readable unitless line height and bounded measure, create paragraph rhythm, and test user text-spacing overrides plus zoom.
Independent typography release decision case 28: increase letter spacing on all paragraphs until every line reaches the same width.

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 29: ellipsis is safe for any content because users understand hidden text exists.

Independent typography release decision case 29: allow natural wrapping, add language-aware hyphenation or emergency token breaks, and keep essential text fully available without fixed-height clipping.
Independent typography release decision case 29: set white-space nowrap and text-overflow ellipsis on every heading to preserve card alignment.

options.text: Independent typography release decision case 30: viewport units alone are ideal because they always scale with device size.
Independent typography release decision case 30: use relative lower and upper bounds, a modest fluid component, and content tests across viewport, root-size, and zoom changes.
Independent typography release decision case 30: remove maximum bounds so display headings can fill all available space.
